evidence_quotes.quote: I want the right to play video games that were sold to me, even if a game publisher decides they no longer support said game.

paragraphs.text: I want to bring attention to a pattern that has sprung up lately. How third party groups, like credit card companies or activist groups, want to influence free speech by dictating what a consumer can or cannot purchase from stores offering legal goods. The legality should be determined by the law, not by disingenuous groups. I also want to voice my concerns regarding online safety acts, that can be easily used to repress the democratic process of free opinion building, by silencing anybody that opposes ruling parties, uses strong language or doesnt support the popular narrative. Lastly I want to address the importance of initiatives like stop killing games. That initiative has reached over 1.4 Million signatures, making it evident that a lot of people care about the issue of selling a product that can be revoked at any given time, for seemingly no reason. Comparable to buying a car and after 5 years the manufacturer disables the engine. In summary, I want the DFA to include the following protections for consumers. I want the right to buy what I want, from an online store that can sell what it wants (within the law of course!). I want the protection of free speech on the internet. Parents should be responsible for the consumption of their children, the collective of the internet. I want the right to play video games that were sold to me, even if a game publisher decides they no longer support said game. Sincerely Elias Kainz
